transition_condition.prompt: Received the user's first name and last name and date of birth
User declines to give any information

instruction.text:
Collect the user's first name, last name and date of birth.

Say something like: "I'd be happy to help you schedule an appointment! 
Can I get your full name and date of birth?"

If the user provides only their first name and last name, ask for their date of birth.
If the user provides only their date of birth, ask for their first and last name.

If the user is hesitant or refuses:
- Reassure them: "No worries, we just need that to look up your record 
  or create one for you."
- If they still decline, say: "That's okay, we can get that when you 
  come in. Let me continue with the other details."

Do NOT ask more than twice for information the user has declined to provide.

transition_condition.prompt: Name and date of birth received
User wants to try again or provide more information
User is done and wants to end the call

instruction.text:
The user was unable or unwilling to provide their first name, last name and/or date of birth.
 
Politely let them know that you may need this information to complete the booking, 
and offer alternatives:
- They can call back when they have the information ready

Ask if there's anything else you can help with.
If they still decline after being reassured, let them know that's okay and move on.
Do not ask more than two times total for any piece of information.
